item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reading your comment put me in a good mood for today.
Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the very nice comment; I'm glad to hear you enjoyed the read!

I actually didn't know about the term "metempsychosis" until now. Thank you for broadening my horizons!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this. I read it in the morning and it put me in a good mood for the day.

I feel that it’s a particularly elegant execution of an emotive concept. I appreciate the way it builds on existing ideas of metempsychosis, and the direction it takes them. The mechanics and thematics are interwoven excellently. I really like the images too.

item.title: Re: Co-Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Aww, no I didn't!

yes you did ;-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! We really appreciate your taking a close look at the draft during the editing stage!

item.title: Re: Co-Author Post
item.content:encoded:
(Zyn basically wrote the entire article)

Aww, no I didn't! I just had a bunch of ideas. I really liked the idea when I reviewed the draft in the forum: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-9788834/mulvis-hexagonum-working-title and I was quite excited to write more for it. AlleraRey found all the images and was wonderful to work with, especially since we didn't use chat for communication at all!

Big thanks to everyone in chat who read this over before it went live! Also ViaNegativa and SpacemanFive, who read this right as it was posted!

There was a lot of background reading that went into this, mostly Wikipedia articles. A few Easter eggs:

+ show block
– hide block
  • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hawksbill_sea_turtle#Indo-Pacific_subpopulation
  • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sea_Turtle_Association_of_Japan,_Kuroshima_Research_Station
  • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Omamori
  • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ry%C5%ABjin ("Sea turtles, fish and jellyfish are often depicted as Ryūjin's servants.")
  • the numbers 4 and 6 are references to the 4 Types of Reincarnation and 6 Realms of Rebirth from Buddhism.

item.title: Co-Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This article is the result of the co authorship between me and Zyn. (Zyn basically wrote the entire article)

Thank you for the following reviewers, Pratten, Lazar, Meserach, bluesoul, nulljellyfish

Special thanks to rounderhouse for line-by-line review and specific wording fixes!

All images except for the baby turtle is mine.

The turtle image is from, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Tartaruga_de_pente.jpg
and is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-Share Alike 4.0 International license.
